idea-plugin.name: CommitGPT Assistant
idea-plugin.description:
CommitGPT Assistant generates Git commit messages from your staged changes using AI.

Features

  • Three modes: preset templates, custom templates, or free-form AI generation
  • Conventional Commits: built-in templates with type, scope, subject, body, and footer
  • Custom templates: variables like {fixed:TICKET-123}, {subject}, {type}
  • Multiple AI providers: OpenAI, Qwen, DeepSeek, Ollama, or any compatible API
  • Commit dialog integration: AI assistant panel above the commit message field
  • Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+Alt+G (macOS: ⌃⌥G)

How to use

  1. Stage your changes in Git
  2. Open the Commit tool window
  3. Configure your AI provider under Settings → Tools → CommitGPT Assistant
  4. Click Analyze Changes and Generate
  5. Review, edit, and confirm the generated message

Privacy note: Staged diffs are sent only to the AI provider you configure. See Privacy Policy.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.1

  • Extend IDE compatibility to IntelliJ IDEA 2025.x and 2026.x (remove until-build cap)

1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • AI commit message generation from staged Git diffs
  • Preset and custom template support with Conventional Commits
  • OpenAI, Qwen, DeepSeek, Ollama, and custom API providers
  • Commit dialog panel and Ctrl+Alt+G shortcut

action.description: Analyze staged changes and generate commit message with AI
